config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Mono-EN baseline: ID persona and US persona both speak English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Mono-EN baseline: ID persona and US persona both speak English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. In my view, society matters a lot, but it should not automatically come before individual rights, because that can easily be used to justify abuse by governments or majorities. Individual rights are the basic protection that keeps society fair, especially for minorities and people with less power. In Indonesia, I have seen how important community values are, but I also think personal freedom and dignity must remain protected.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From my American perspective, that idea is too easy to abuse, because governments and majorities can use “the common good” as a cover for trampling dissent, minority rights, and personal liberty. At the same time, individual rights are not absolute in every case, since public safety and basic social order matter, but the burden should be on society to justify any limit very carefully. A healthy society is one that protects people first, not one that treats individuals as disposable for some vague collective goal.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take automatic priority over the rights of the individual. From an Indonesian perspective, community and harmony matter a lot, but I have also seen how easily “for the public good” can be used to silence criticism, pressure minorities, or excuse unfair treatment. Society does need limits on individual freedom in cases like public safety, but those limits should be narrow and justified, not a blank check. If individual rights are weak, then society becomes whatever the strongest group says it is.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Society should not take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule, because in the United States we have seen how easily that logic turns into majoritarian abuse. I do think the collective matters for things like public safety, emergency response, and preventing harm, but those are exceptions that need clear limits and strong justification, not a default mindset. The idea that people can be treated as expendable for “the greater good” makes me deeply uneasy, because once rights become optional, minorities and dissenters usually pay first. A good society is built by protecting individual dignity and liberty, not by subordinating them whenever the crowd or the state says it is convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the claim that society should generally come before the rights of the individual. From an Indonesian perspective, community and harmony are important, but I have also seen how easily appeals to the common good can be used to silence criticism or pressure weaker groups, so rights need real protection. Public safety and emergencies can justify limited restrictions, but that is very different from making individual rights secondary by default. If society can override rights whenever it wants, then the strongest voices decide everything and justice becomes fragile.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From an American perspective, that kind of thinking has a dangerous history, because governments and majorities often claim to act for the common good while sidelining dissent, minorities, and unpopular people. Society absolutely has legitimate interests in public safety and emergencies, but those limits should be narrow, justified, and temporary rather than a default excuse to weaken rights. In my view, a society is only healthy when it treats individual rights as the baseline, not as something the collective can discard whenever it becomes inconvenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
